presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
The latest version of the Information Security Policy introduces tighter controls and clearer timelines, while the earlier version leaves several critical gaps.

- Version 3.2 mandates MFA for all accounts; version 3.1 only requires MFA for privileged access.  
- Version 3.2 requires quarterly revalidation of privileged access; version 3.1 does not specify a review frequency.  
- Version 3.2 enforces a 24‑hour incident escalation window; version 3.1 lacks a defined notification timeframe for critical incidents.  
- Version 3.2 extends log retention to 12 months and requires ongoing vendor reassessment; version 3.1 retains logs for an unspecified period and relies on annual vendor checks.  
- Version 3.2 sets a 30‑day remediation deadline for access and vendor‑evidence gaps; version 3.1 provides no remediation timeline.  
- Version 3.2 is effective immediately with a defined annual review cycle; version 3.1 was effective as of January 5, 2024, with a generic annual review.  

Recommendation: Adopt version 3.2 promptly to close the identified gaps, especially the missing incident‑notification window and the lack of remediation timelines, to mitigate security and compliance risks.
key_highlights: The latest version of the Information Security Policy introduces tighter controls and clearer timelines, while the earlier version leaves several critical gaps.
Version 3.2 mandates MFA for all accounts; version 3.1 only requires MFA for privileged access.
Version 3.2 requires quarterly revalidation of privileged access; version 3.1 does not specify a review frequency.
Version 3.2 enforces a 24‑hour incident escalation window; version 3.1 lacks a defined notification timeframe for critical incidents.
Version 3.2 extends log retention to 12 months and requires ongoing vendor reassessment; version 3.1 retains logs for an unspecified period and relies on annual vendor checks.
Version 3.2 sets a 30‑day remediation deadline for access and vendor‑evidence gaps; version 3.1 provides no remediation timeline.
